tokenBalances.description: An array of BatchGetTokenBalanceOutputItem objects returned by the response.

errors.description: An array of BatchGetTokenBalanceErrorItem objects returned from the request.

address.description: The contract or wallet address for the owner.

tokenIdentifier.description:
The container for the identifier for the token including the unique token ID and its blockchain network.

Only the native tokens BTC and ETH, and the ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC 1155 token standards are supported.

tokenId.description:
The unique identifier of the token.

For native tokens, use the 3 character abbreviation that best matches your token. For example, btc for Bitcoin, eth for Ether, etc. For all other token types you must specify the tokenId in the 64 character hexadecimal tokenid format.

time.description:
The container of the Timestamp of the blockchain instant.

This timestamp will only be recorded up to the second.

The container of the Timestamp of the blockchain instant.

This timestamp will only be recorded up to the second.

contractIdentifier.description: Contains the blockchain address and network information about the contract.

tokenStandard.description: The token standard of the contract requested.

deployerAddress.description: The address of the deployer of contract.

contractAddress.description: Container for the blockchain address about a contract.

tokenIdentifier.description:
The container for the identifier for the token including the unique token ID and its blockchain network.

Only the native tokens BTC and ETH, and the ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC 1155 token standards are supported.

address.description: The contract or wallet address for the owner.

network.description: The blockchain network where the transaction occurred.

blockHash.description: The block hash is a unique identifier for a block. It is a fixed-size string that is calculated by using the information in the block. The block hash is used to verify the integrity of the data in the block.

transactionHash.description: The hash of a transaction. It is generated when a transaction is created.

blockNumber.description: The block number in which the transaction is recorded.

transactionIndex.description: The index of the transaction within a blockchain.

numberOfTransactions.description: The number of transactions in the block.

to.description: The identifier of the transaction. It is generated whenever a transaction is verified and added to the blockchain.

from.description: The initiator of the transaction. It is either in the form a public key or a contract address.

gasUsed.description: The amount of gas used for the transaction.

cumulativeGasUsed.description: The amount of gas used up to the specified point in the block.

signatureV.description: The signature of the transaction. The Z coordinate of a point V.

signatureR.description: The signature of the transaction. The X coordinate of a point R.

signatureS.description: The signature of the transaction. The Y coordinate of a point S.

transactionId.description: The identifier of a Bitcoin transaction. It is generated when a transaction is created.

confirmationStatus.description: Specifies whether the transaction has reached Finality.

executionStatus.description: Identifies whether the transaction has succeeded or failed.

contracts.description: An array of contract objects that contain the properties for each contract.

nextToken.description: The pagination token that indicates the next set of results to retrieve.

contractIdentifier.description: The container for the contract identifier containing its blockchain network and address.

contractAddress.description: Container for the blockchain address about a contract.

events.description: The transaction events returned by the request.

nextToken.description: The pagination token that indicates the next set of results to retrieve.

network.description: The blockchain network where the transaction occurred.

transactionHash.description: The hash of a transaction. It is generated when a transaction is created.

from.description: The wallet address initiating the transaction. It can either be a public key or a contract.

to.description: The wallet address receiving the transaction. It can either be a public key or a contract.

tokenId.description: The unique identifier for the token involved in the transaction.

transactionId.description: The identifier of a Bitcoin transaction. It is generated when a transaction is created.

voutIndex.description: The position of the transaction output in the transaction output list.

voutSpent.description:
Specifies if the transaction output is spent or unspent. This is only returned for BITCOIN_VOUT event types.

This is only returned for BITCOIN_VOUT event types.

spentVoutTransactionId.description:
The transactionId that created the spent transaction output.

This is only returned for BITCOIN_VIN event types.

spentVoutTransactionHash.description:
The transactionHash that created the spent transaction output.

This is only returned for BITCOIN_VIN event types.

spentVoutIndex.description:
The position of the spent transaction output in the output list of the creating transaction.

This is only returned for BITCOIN_VIN event types.

time.description:
The container of the Timestamp of the blockchain instant.

This timestamp will only be recorded up to the second.

confirmationStatus.description: This container specifies whether the transaction has reached Finality.

tokenBalances.description: An array of TokenBalance objects. Each object contains details about the token balance.

nextToken.description: The pagination token that indicates the next set of results to retrieve.

atBlockchainInstant.description:
The time for when the TokenBalance is requested or the current time if a time is not provided in the request.

This time will only be recorded up to the second.

lastUpdatedTime.description: The Timestamp of the last transaction at which the balance for the token in the wallet was updated.
